item.title: 让我们学习 11ty 第 6 部分：插件 | Let's Learn 11ty Part 6: Plugins
item.description:
在上一篇文章中，我们部署了我们的站点。 在这一点上，它对于一个网站来说工作得很好。 然而，我们可以做更多的事情来改进它的功能 - 从插件开始

插件

插件是可以导入 Eleventy 以实现附加功能的外部代码。

由于 Eleventy 的性质，官方（在@11ty前缀下）和社区都贡献了插件。

下面是一些官方插件：

  • 图像：允许调整大小和生成图像
  • 语法高亮：允许在没有客户端 JavaScript 的情况下高亮代码语法。
  • 导航：在您的站点中创建分层导航。

要使用插件，例如 Image one：

 npm install @11ty/eleventy-img

然后将其包含在顶部的.eleventy.js中：

 const Image = require ( " @11ty/eleventy-img " );

其余插件可在此页面查看

使用插件改进我们的网站

改善搜索引擎优化

我们网站的 SEO 目前还不是很好。 我们可以做几个步骤来改进它。

让我们使用其中一个内置插件来帮助我们创建robots.txt和 `sitemap.xml

机器人

在我们的src中，我们将在其中创建一个robots.txt文件：

仅仅创建这个文件不会做任何事情。 你还记得为什么吗？

注意： .txt不是支持的模板语言的一部分，我们必须为我们的文件添加 PassThroughCopy

在.eleventy.js中，添加：

网站地图

当我们还在这个文件中时，让我们为 out 站点地图添加一些代码。

在文件顶部，执行以下操作：

js const { DateTime } = require("luxon");
然后

在上面的代码中，我们从luxon导入DateTime - 它内置于 Eleventy

然后，我们为当前日期创建一个简码，并使用DateTime来获取我们将在站点地图中使用的当前日期

现在我们可以制作我们的站点地图文件了。 在src创建一个sitemap.njk文件

以上：

  • 我们使用永久链接键来指定我们希望如何呈现这个文件
  • 此外，我们将其从我们的收藏中排除
  • 我们在所有集合上运行一个循环
    • 然后我们输出我们网站的 URL 以及特定的页面 url - page.url
    • 我们使用currentDate短代码输出每个循环通过的页面被修改的日期

注意：确保将站点 url 替换为您自己部署的 URL

在帖子上显示日期

我们将再次使用DateTime ，但有点不同：

将blog.njk更改为如下所示：


我添加了更多类以使博客主页看起来更好

结论

今天我们学习了 Eleventy 中的 Plugins。 他们如何帮助我们在 Eleventy 网站上完成几件事。

我们使用了一个插件来：

  • 添加站点地图
  • 显示我们帖子的日期

还不错吧？ 到目前为止，我们还没有导入任何东西。 即使是现在，我们添加的也没有那么多

一如既往：

感谢您的阅读，让我们连接！

感谢您访问我的这个小角落。 让我们在Twitter 、 Polywork和LinkedIn上联系



原文:

In our last article we deployed our site. At this point, it is working quite well for a site. There is however more we can do to improve how it functions - starting with Plugins

Plugins

Plugins are pieces of external code that can be imported into Eleventy to implement additional functionality.

Owing to Eleventy's nature, there both official (under the @11ty prefix) and community contributed plugins.

Below are some official plugins:

  • Edge: Allows you to run Eleventy in a Edge Function for dynamic addition of content to your Eleventy site.
  • Image: To allow resizing and generation of images
  • Syntax Highlighting: To permit code syntax highlighting without client-side JavaScript.
  • Navigation: To create hierarchical navigation in your site.

To use a plugin, say for example the Image one:

npm install @11ty/eleventy-img

Then include it in your .eleventy.js at the top:

const Image = require("@11ty/eleventy-img");

The rest of the plugins can be viewed on this page here

Using A Plugin to Improve Our Site

Improve SEO

The SEO for our site is not that good currently. We can do a few steps to improve it.

Let's use one of the built in plugins to help us create a robots.txt and `sitemap.xml

Robots

In our src , we'll make a robots.txt file with this in it:

Just creating this file won't do anything. Do you recall why?

NOTE: .txt is not part of the supported template languages, we have to add a PassThroughCopy for our file

In .eleventy.js, add this:

Sitemap

While we're still in this file, let's add some code for out sitemap.

At the top of the file, do this:

js const { DateTime } = require("luxon");
Then

In the code above, we import DateTime from luxon - which is built in to Eleventy

We then create a shortcode for the current date and use DateTime to get the current date which we'll use in our sitemap

Now we can make our sitemap file. In src create a sitemap.njk file

Above:

  • We used the permalink key to specify how we want this file to be rendered
  • In addition, we exclude it from our collections
  • We run a loop on all of our collections
    • We then output the URL of our site along with the particular page url - page.url
    • We use our currentDate shortcode to output the date when each page looped through was modified

NOTE: Make sure you replace the site url with your own deployed URL

Display Date on Posts

We will make use of DateTime once more, but a bit differently:

The change blog.njk to look like this:


I have added more classes to make the blog home look better

Conclusion

Today we have learnt about Plugins in Eleventy. How they can help us accomplish several things on our Eleventy site.

We have used a plugin to:

  • add a sitemap
  • display the date of our posts

Not bad huh? Up until this point we hadn't imported anything. And even now, what we have added is not that much

As always:

Thank you for reading, let's connect!

Thank you for visiting this little corner of mine. Let's connect on Twitter, Polywork and LinkedIn

原文:

Mostafa Said

Posted on Jun 14

Hey awesome developers! 👋

I'm currently working on backend for a complex project that will require many tables and relationships. I was advised to create UML Diagram and UML use case before I start building anything in the project.

I usually start building, improvise and hope it works 😄

So I'm wondering, do you guys usually create UML diagrams before building new projects? Or are you like me?

Toheeb Ogunbiyi

Do you still wonder when to use px, em, or rem?

You need to understand how they differ by responsiveness and not simplicity.

For that, I made a tool to show you how px, em, and rem relates to its component, document, and the user's preference in the browser settings.

Here's the tool

The tweet below has a video of me using it. (+ why I made it)

Should 3rd party stylesheets use #CSS em or rem?rem offers one source of truth which is great for simplicity in calculating valuesBUT, clients can't scope rem values to a page component as em doesWhich is the greater good? em's flexibility or rem's simplicity?

#cssday

If you've got any questions, hit me up in the comments ✌️.

item.title: 使用 NGINX 优化图像以提高 Web 性能 | Optimizing Images for Web Performance with NGINX
item.description:
在开发网站时，图像一直是痛苦的根源。 为了最大限度地提高 Web 性能，开发人员必须考虑多种格式和分辨率。 您通常会以不同大小和格式对相同图像进行笛卡尔爆炸以支持不同的场景。

例如，您不想将用于高 DPI 屏幕的高分辨率图像发送到低 DPI 屏幕 - 您会浪费带宽和刻录时间。 使用正确的文件格式同样重要。 自动消除痛苦

在您的网站上创建您需要的每个图像的所有排列可能既耗时又烦人。 在 Request Metrics，我们有一个 Photoshop 模板，可以为我们完成一些导出工作，但这仍然是一个手动步骤。

相反，如果我们可以让 NGINX 为我们完成繁重的工作，并动态创建必要的图像会怎样？

免责声明：以下内容更多的是概念证明，而不是最佳实践。 就像他们在侏罗纪公园说的：

你们的科学家们如此专注于他们是否可以，他们没有停下来思考他们是否应该这样做。

一张图片统治他们

这是我们的网络性能指南中的一张大英雄图片：

此图像宽 2000 像素，适用于高 DPI 屏幕，如 Apple 视网膜显示器上的屏幕。 我们经常使用 PNG 格式，因为它受到创意工具和浏览器的广泛支持。

问题是这张图片很大——无论是分辨率还是文件大小——都只有350kb 。

如果浏览器支持，我们希望能够使用srcset属性指定 1x 和 2x WebP 图像，如果不支持，则回退到 PNG：

 <!-- If the browser supports webp, let's use that --> <!-- NOTE: we also want high and low res versions! --> <img srcset= "/images/header.1x.webp 1x, /images/header.webp 2x" src= "/images/header.webp" /> <!-- If the browser does *not** support webp, fallback to PNG --> <img srcset= "/images/header.1x.png 1x, /images/header.png 2x" src= "/images/header.png" />

本质上，我们需要相同图像的四个版本： PNG和Webp格式的图像的高分辨率和低分辨率版本。 但是我们只有一个高分辨率 PNG，为网站上我们想要的每张图片制作其他三个版本是很烦人的。 这不就是计算机的用途——为我们做无聊的工作吗？

您甚至可以创建采用自定义宽度和高度值的端点。

这很酷，但我们不想考虑图像大小。 我们只想要一个是原始分辨率一半的1x ，不管那个尺寸是多少。 所以它也没有让我们更接近我们的WebP格式目标！

我们真正需要的是ImageMagick 之类的东西。 它是一个可以读取图像属性（如宽度和高度）、调整大小甚至更改格式的库。 一把名副其实的瑞士军刀，用于图像。 但是我们如何从 NGINX 中访问它呢？ 好吧，有点诡计。

有大量的绑定库可以支持几乎所有编程语言的 ImageMagick。 幸运的是，有人为 Lua 构建了一个。 嗯，我们为什么要关心 Lua，这如何让我们更接近我们的目标？

它通常不是开箱即用的，但很容易通过简单的模块安装来添加。 一旦我们有了 Lua 支持，我们就可以使用我们发现的 Lua-to-ImageMagick 绑定！ 或者至少，这是理论。

在 Ubuntu-land 中，要启用对 NGINX 的 Lua 支持，请使用以下命令：

 # Note: Other package managers have different names for the Lua module apt-get install libnginx-mod-http-lua

与 NGINX 的 ImageMagick 交谈

我们已经在 NGINX 中启用了 Lua 支持，但我们还没有完成。 如果我们查看 ImageMagick Lua 绑定的安装说明，我们需要更多的依赖项。

 apt-get install luajit #<-- should be installed with Lua module apt-get install libmagickwand-dev #<-- static libs for ImageMagick API apt-get install luarocks #<-- Lua package manager luarocks install magick #<-- Install the actual lua binding

所以现在我们已经有了所有的依赖关系，我们应该能够直接从 NGINX 与 ImageMagick 对话。 让我们看看我们能想出什么样的坏主意！

自动将 PNG 转换为 WebP

对于我们所有的图像，我们希望能够自动将它们从 PNG 转换为 WebP。 我们可以在 NGINX 中创建一个特殊的位置来为我们做这件事！

主要目的是我们将在 NGINX 中设置一个特殊位置来处理对.webp图像的所有请求。 如果图像存在于磁盘上，很好，我们将使用try_files的第一个参数返回它。 如果没有，那么我们将寻找同名的 PNG 并尝试将其即时转换为 WebP！

 # Handle any requests for .webp images. # We first look for the file on disk, and if it's not present, # call our internal location that does the conversion for us. location ~* / images /(?< filename >.+). webp { try_files $ uri @ webp ; } # Internal-only location that will convert an existing PNG to WebP # And save that WebP image to disk for future requests location @ webp { content_by_lua_block { local magick = require ( "magick" ) local rootDir = '/var/www/images/' local srcImgPath = rootDir .. ngx . var . filename .. '.png' local img = magick . load_image ( srcImgPath ) if not img then ngx . status = 404 ngx . exit ( 0 ) end img : set_format ( 'webp' ) img : set_quality ( 100 ) local destFileName = ngx . var . filename .. '.webp' local destImgPath = rootDir .. destFileName img : write ( destImgPath ) ngx . exec ( "/images/" .. destFileName ) } }

这里有一点要解压。 让我们从顶级位置开始。

 location ~* / images /(?< filename >.+). webp { try_files $ uri @ webp ; }

此位置处理对.webp图像的所有请求。 它首先在磁盘上查找文件，如果找不到，它会委托给一个名为@webp的备用内部位置。

 location @ webp { content_by_lua_block { ...

然后您可能会注意到content_by_lua_block指令。 这让我们可以直接在 NGINX conf 中执行任意 Lua 代码。 在 NGINX 请求/响应生命周期的各个地方都有一些这样的指令，并且有些版本在磁盘上获取文件而不是内联代码。

 local magick = require ( "magick" ) local rootDir = '/var/www/images/' local srcImgPath = rootDir .. ngx . var . filename .. '.png' local img = magick . load_image ( srcImgPath ) if not img then ngx . status = 404 ngx . exit ( 0 ) end

接下来我们加载我们安装的 ImageMagick 绑定并使用它在磁盘上查找现有的 PNG 文件。 如果我们没有找到要转换的现有 PNG 文件，我们使用内部ngx对象来创建 404 响应。

 img : set_format ( 'webp' ) img : set_quality ( 100 ) local destFileName = ngx . var . filename .. '.webp' local destImgPath = rootDir .. destFileName img : write ( destImgPath )

如果我们有图像，我们将格式设置为webp ，将质量设置为100 ，然后使用.webp文件扩展名重新保存它。 这有效地将 PNG 转换为具有无损压缩的 WebP。

 ... ngx . exec ( "/images/" .. destFileName )

最后，我们使用ngx.exec()在内部重定向到我们新保存的 WebP 文件！ 随后的请求将跳过所有这些 Lua 代码，只从磁盘获取文件。

更进一步

我们现在可以即时将 PNG 转换为 WebP，并且性能良好！ 但我们也希望在需要时自动创建较低分辨率的版本。 我们可以在一个新的 Lua 块中添加几行代码来实现这一点。

制作 1x 图像

当我们说1x或2x图像时，我们实际上是在谈论devicePixelRatio 。 即一个CSS像素与一个物理像素的比率。 所以，假设我们的网站是 CSS 中定义的 1000px 宽。 在具有 2 倍设备像素比的 Retina 设备上，我们需要一个 2000 像素宽的图像来填充该空间，而无需浏览器对其进行放大。

但是，如果用户不使用高分辨率显示器，最好向他们发送图像的“1x”版本——即 1000 像素宽的图像。 这将节省网络带宽和时间。 由于我们只想处理单个图像，所以我们只制作高分辨率版本，我们会让 NGINX 自动将其调整为较低的分辨率。

在我们的站点配置中，我们可以执行以下操作：

 # Look for any images with a {filename}.1x.{extension} # We'll support PNG *or* WebP for fun location ~* / images /(?< filename >.+). 1 x .(?< extension > png | webp ) { try_files $ uri @ 1 x ; } location @ 1 x { content_by_lua_block { #... same directory setup and 404 returning code as before if ngx . var . extension == 'webp' then img : set_format ( 'webp' ) img : set_quality ( 100 ) end local imgWidth = img : get_width () local imgHeight = img : get_height () local destFileName = ngx . var . filename .. '.1x.' .. ngx . var . extension local destImgPath = rootDir .. destFileName img : resize ( imgWidth / 2 , imgHeight / 2 ) img : write ( destImgPath ) ngx . exec ( "/images/" .. destFileName ) } }

与前面代码的主要区别在于我们询问图像的宽度和高度，然后我们将这些值除以 2 以生成1x图像。

 local imgWidth = img : get_width () local imgHeight = img : get_height () img : resize ( imgWidth / 2 , imgHeight / 2 )

结果

我们原来的 PNG是350kb 。 生成的1x PNG是一个小得多的204kb 。

配置您的 NGINX 静态内容服务器为您进行图像转换并不需要太多工作。 并且“免费”获得 2-3 倍更小的有效载荷是每个人都会感到兴奋的事情！

这有什么不同吗？

找出答案的唯一方法是使用像Request Metrics这样的产品来衡量您网站的实际性能数字。 了解实际用户的体验以及您的更改是否有所作为！



原文:

Images are a constant source of pain when developing websites. There are many formats and resolutions a developer must consider in order to maximize web performance. You’ll often end up with a cartesian explosion of the same image in different sizes and formats to support different scenarios.

For example, you don’t want to send a high res image meant for high DPI screens to a low DPI screen - you’d be wasting bandwidth and burning time. Using the right file format is equally important. WebP is usually the best choice since it keeps size down, but not all browsers support it.

Automating the Pain Away

Creating all the permutations of every image you need on your site can be time consuming and irritating. At Request Metrics we have a Photoshop template that will do some of the work for us on export, but it’s still a manual step.

What if, instead, we could let NGINX do the heavy lifting for us, and create the necessary images on the fly?

Disclaimer: What follows is more of a proof of concept than a best practice. Like they say in Jurassic Park:

Your scientists were so preoccupied with whether or not they could, they didn’t stop to think if they should.

One Image to Rule Them All

Here’s a big hero image from our web performance guide:

This image is 2000px wide and meant to be used for high DPI screens, like those found on Apple’s retina displays. We often work in PNG format because it’s widely supported by creative tools and browsers.

The problem is this image is huge - both in terms of resolution and file size - weighing in at 350kb.

We’d like to be able to use the srcset property to specify 1x and 2x WebP images if the browser supports it, or fallback to PNG if not:

<!-- If the browser supports webp, let's use that -->
<!-- NOTE: we also want high and low res versions! -->
<img srcset="/images/header.1x.webp 1x, /images/header.webp 2x" src="/images/header.webp" /> <!-- If the browser does *not** support webp, fallback to PNG -->
<img srcset="/images/header.1x.png 1x, /images/header.png 2x" src="/images/header.png" />

Essentially, we need four versions of the same image: high res and low res versions of the image in both PNG and Webp format. But we only have a single high res PNG and it’s a lot of irritation to make the other three versions for every image we want on the site. Isn’t this what computers are for - to do the boring work for us?

NGINX Image Module to the Rescue?

NGINX has long had an image module which can be used to resize images on the fly. You can even create endpoints that take custom width and height values.

This is cool, but we don’t want to think about image sizes. We just want a 1x that is half the resolution of the original, whatever that size might be. The NGINX image module also does not support changing file formats. So it doesn’t get us any closer to our WebP format goal either!

ImageMagick can do Everything

What we really need is something like ImageMagick. It’s a library that can read image attributes like width and height, do resizing, and even change format. A veritable swiss army knife for images. But how can we access it from within NGINX? Well, with a bit of trickery.

Lua Bindings for ImageMagick

There’s a large number of binding libraries out there to support ImageMagick from almost any programming language. Luckily there is someone who built one for Lua. Ermm, why do we care about Lua and how does this get us closer to our goal?

The NGINX Lua Module

NGINX has supported Lua as a scripting language for some time. It’s not usually enabled out of the box, but it’s easy enough to add with a simple module install. And once we have Lua support, we can use that fancy Lua-to-ImageMagick binding we found! Or at least, that’s the theory.

In Ubuntu-land, to enable Lua support for NGINX, use this:

 # Note: Other package managers have different names for the Lua module
apt-get install libnginx-mod-http-lua

Talking to ImageMagick from NGINX

We’ve got Lua support enabled in NGINX, but we’re not quite done. If we look at the installation instructions for the ImageMagick Lua binding, we need a few more dependencies.

apt-get install luajit #<-- should be installed with Lua module
apt-get install libmagickwand-dev #<-- static libs for ImageMagick API
apt-get install luarocks #<-- Lua package manager luarocks install magick #<-- Install the actual lua binding

So now that we’ve got all the dependencies, we should be able to talk to ImageMagick directly from NGINX. Let’s see what sort of bad ideas we can come up with!

Converting PNG to WebP Automatically

For all of our images, we’d like the ability to convert them from PNG to WebP automatically. We can create a special location in NGINX to do exactly that for us!

The main thrust is that we’ll set up a special location in NGINX that handles all requests for .webp images. If the image exists on disk, great, we’ll just return it using the first argument of try_files. If it doesn’t, then we’ll look for a PNG of the same name and try and convert it to WebP on the fly!

# Handle any requests for .webp images.
# We first look for the file on disk, and if it's not present,
# call our internal location that does the conversion for us.
location ~* /images/(?<filename>.+).webp { try_files $uri @webp;
} # Internal-only location that will convert an existing PNG to WebP
# And save that WebP image to disk for future requests
location @webp { content_by_lua_block { local magick = require("magick") local rootDir = '/var/www/images/' local srcImgPath = rootDir .. ngx.var.filename .. '.png' local img = magick.load_image(srcImgPath) if not img then ngx.status = 404 ngx.exit(0) end img:set_format('webp') img:set_quality(100) local destFileName = ngx.var.filename .. '.webp' local destImgPath = rootDir .. destFileName img:write(destImgPath) ngx.exec("/images/" .. destFileName)
    }
}

There’s a bit to unpack here. Let’s start with the top-level location.

location ~* /images/(?<filename>.+).webp { try_files $uri @webp;
}

This location handles all requests to .webp images. It first looks for the file on disk, and if it doesn’t find it, it delegates to a fallback internal location called @webp.

location @webp { content_by_lua_block {
    ...

You may then notice the content_by_lua_block directive. This lets us execute arbitrary Lua code right inside the NGINX conf. There are a few of these directives at various places in the NGINX request/response lifecycle, and there are versions that take a file on disk instead of inline code.

 local magick = require("magick") local rootDir = '/var/www/images/' local srcImgPath = rootDir .. ngx.var.filename .. '.png' local img = magick.load_image(srcImgPath) if not img then ngx.status = 404 ngx.exit(0) end

Next up we load up the ImageMagick binding that we installed and use it look for an existing PNG file on disk. If we don’t find an existing PNG file to convert, we use the intrinsic ngx object to create a 404 response.

 img:set_format('webp') img:set_quality(100) local destFileName = ngx.var.filename .. '.webp' local destImgPath = rootDir .. destFileName img:write(destImgPath)

If we have an image, we set the format to webp and quality to 100 and re-save it with the .webp file extension. This effectively converts the PNG to WebP with lossless compression.

 ... ngx.exec("/images/" .. destFileName)

Finally we use ngx.exec() to internally redirect to our newly saved WebP file! Subsequent requests will skip all this Lua code and just be served the file from disk.

Taking it Further

We can now convert PNG to WebP on the fly, and with good performance! But we also want to create lower res versions automatically when required. We can accomplish this with a few more lines of code in a new Lua block.

Making 1x Images

When we say 1x or 2x images we’re really talking about the devicePixelRatio. That is, the ratio of one CSS pixel to one physical pixel. So, let’s say our website is 1000px wide as defined in CSS. On a retina device with a 2x device pixel ratio, we need a 2000px wide image to fill that space without the browser upscaling it.

However, if the user isn’t using a high resolution display, it would be better to send them the “1x” version of the image - one that is 1000px wide. This will save bandwidth and time on the wire. Since we only want to deal with a single image, we only make a high resolution version, and we’ll let NGINX resize it to the lower resolution automatically.

Inside our site configuration we can do something like this:

# Look for any images with a {filename}.1x.{extension}
# We'll support PNG *or* WebP for fun
location ~* /images/(?<filename>.+).1x.(?<extension>png|webp) { try_files $uri @1x;
} location @1x { content_by_lua_block { #... same directory setup and 404 returning code as before
 if ngx.var.extension == 'webp' then img:set_format('webp') img:set_quality(100) end local imgWidth = img:get_width() local imgHeight = img:get_height() local destFileName = ngx.var.filename .. '.1x.' .. ngx.var.extension local destImgPath = rootDir .. destFileName img:resize(imgWidth / 2, imgHeight / 2) img:write(destImgPath) ngx.exec("/images/" .. destFileName)
    }
}

The main difference from the previous code is that we interrogate the image for it’s width and height, and then we divide those values by two to make a 1x image.

 local imgWidth = img:get_width() local imgHeight = img:get_height() img:resize(imgWidth / 2, imgHeight / 2)

The Results

Our original PNG was 350kb. The resulting 1x PNG is a much smaller 204kb.

The WebP images are even more compact! The 2x image is only 110kb , and the 1x image is 108kb (not nearly as big of a savings due to the nature of WebP’s compression).

It’s not much work to configure your NGINX static content server to do image conversions for you. The Lua script is only executed once per image so there should not be undue pressure on the server itself. And getting 2-3x smaller payloads for “free” is something everyone can get excited about!

Does it Make a Difference?

The only way to find out is to use a product like Request Metrics to measure the real performance numbers for your site. Find out what actual users are experiencing and if your changes are making a difference!
item.author: toddhgardner

item.title: 自动生成 OpenAPI——网络不会说谎！ | Auto OpenAPI Generation — The Network Doesn’t Lie!
item.description:
哈里什·纳塔拉吉

发表于然而，大多数组织无法回答以下关键问题：

  • 我有哪些 API？
  • 是否记录和维护 API 的OpenAPI 模式？
  • 哪些 API 处理敏感数据？
  • 哪些用户在哪些角色（或范围）下访问哪些 API？
  • 哪些 API 是外部的还是内部的？

基于注释的 OpenAPI 生成不可扩展

不同的开源工具集

有大量（特定于语言/框架的）开源工具在构建时通过扫描源代码生成 OpenAPI 规范。

他们使用开发人员添加到语言/框架特定资源、方法和控制器的代码级注释来为 API 生成模式。

这些工具不能很好地扩展

现代开发环境本质上是多语言的，您可以在其中找到几个小团队，使用各种技术堆栈和不同的编程语言。

由于以下原因，这些工具在这些环境中不能很好地扩展：

  • 在如此多的小团队、不同的编程语言和各种语言框架之间，很难实现（API 文档的）一致性。
  • 开发者需要在适当的地方正确注释代码，以免文档不完整和不准确。
  • 开发人员在不断重构代码时需要维护注释，以免文档不完整和不准确。
  • 注释本质上是静态的。 当 API 返回动态对象时，自动记录的规范将不正确或不完整。
  • 无法保证自动记录的内容是 API 展示的实际连线行为。

网络不会说谎

这使得网络成为 API 行为和模式的最终真实来源。

直到最近，以一致且可扩展的方式利用网络来自动发现和自动记录 API 一直是个挑战。

基于 eBPF 的自动 OpenAPI 生成

实现一致性和可扩展性，同时保护隐私

无摩擦且保护隐私的 API 可观察性

安装涉及 Kubernetes 的单个Helm Chart ，或其他环境的单个Docker 命令。 安装不需要对您的应用程序进行代码更改、配置更改或 SDK 添加。

确保客户数据的隐私至关重要。 您可以在此处阅读有关隐私保护功能的更多信息。

使用敏感数据注释自动生成 OpenAPI

运行时环境中的所有活动 API 都会被自动发现，并且 OpenAPI 规范会自动为它们生成。

自动生成的 OpenAPI 规范

持续验证现有 OpenAPI 文档

使用API-First方法构建 API 文档的团队也可以从中受益。

使用自动生成的文档交叉验证现有 OpenAPI 文档的准确性和完整性。

有兴趣尝试 Auto OpenAPI Generation？



原文:

Harish Nataraj

Posted on Jun 14

Visibility of APIs is a fundamental necessity for governance, security, and productivity. Yet most organizations are unable to answer the following critical questions:

  • What APIs do I have?
  • Are OpenAPI schemas for the APIs documented and maintained?
  • Which APIs process sensitive data?
  • Which users access which APIs, under what roles (or scopes)?
  • Which APIs are external vs. internal?

Annotation Based OpenAPI Generation Is Not Scalable

Disparate Set of Open Source Tools

There are a plethora of (language/framework specific) open source tools that generate OpenAPI specifications at build time, by scanning source code.

They use code level annotations added by developers, to language/framework specific resources, methods, and controllers, to generate schema for APIs.

Examples include Swagger-Core, SpringFox, NSwag / Swashbuckle, Swagger-express / HAPI-Swagger, Django-REST-Swagger / Flask-RESTplus, etc.

These Tools Don’t Scale Well

Modern development environments are polyglot in nature, where you find several small teams, using a variety of technology stacks, and disparate programming languages.

These tools don’t scale well in these environments due to the following reasons:

  • It is hard to achieve consistency (of API documentation) across so many small teams, disparate programming languages, and variety of language frameworks.
  • Developers need to correctly annotate the code in appropriate places, lest the documentation will be incomplete and inaccurate.
  • Developers need to maintain the annotations as they continuously refactor code, lest the documentation will be incomplete and inaccurate.
  • Annotations are static in nature. When APIs return dynamic objects, the auto documented specification will be incorrect, or incomplete.
  • There is no guarantee that what is auto documented, is the actual wire behavior exhibited by the API.

The Network Doesn’t Lie

APIs transact by exchanging JSON data over the network. This makes the network, the ultimate source of truth for API behavior, and schema.

Harnessing the network to auto discover & auto document APIs, in a consistent and scalable manner, has been challenging until recently.

eBPF Based Automatic OpenAPI Generation

Achieve Consistency & Scale, while Preserving Privacy

eBPF is revolutionary technology, that enables auto documentation of APIs in a consistent, scalable, and language/framework agnostic manner.

Levo provides a free service that automatically generates OpenAPI documentation for all your APIs, by passively observing your runtime API traffic via eBPF.

The installation involves a single Helm Chart for Kubernetes, or a single Docker command for other environments. The installation does not require code changes, configuration changes, or SDK additions to your applications.

Ensuring the privacy of your customer data is paramount. You can read more about privacy-preserving features here.

Auto Generate OpenAPI with Sensitive Data Annotations

All APIs active in the runtime environments are auto discovered, and OpenAPI specifications are auto generated for them.

OpenAPI specifications are annotated with sensitive data types (PII, PSI, PHI, etc).

The API documentation is always fresh, and is based on the single source of truth, which is the actual API behavior observed on the network.

Continuously Validate Existing OpenAPI Documentation

Teams building API documentation with an API-First approach, can also benefit.

Cross validate existing OpenAPI documentation with auto generated documentation for accuracy and completeness.

Interested in trying Auto OpenAPI Generation?

Levo is a purpose-built, developer-first API security solution that fully automates API Observability, API Documentation & API Testing in CI/CD pipelines.

Signup for a forever free account, and start auto documenting all your APIs within minutes.

Have questions? Contact us at info@levo.ai.

Ashique Billa Molla - Jun 5
item.author: hnataraj

item.title: 反应原生：相机未激活 | React native: Camera is not active
item.description:
我正在为 react-native 开发我的库。 我需要使用相机。
我发现了不错的包react-native-camera-kit和react-native-vision-camera
我使用 create-react-native-library ( https://github.com/callstack/react-native-builder-bob ) 创建了我的库

将react-native-camera-kit添加到devDependencies和peerDependencies 。 向我的应用添加并配置react-native-camera-kit

当我在我的应用程序中使用 react-native-camera-kit 时，一切正常。 我在我的库中使用 react-native-camera-kit 创建了组件并将其导出。

当我尝试使用此组件时出现错误相机已关闭

再次。 直接在应用程序内使用react-native-camera-kit - 好的。 通过我的库使用react-native-camera-kit - 错误

也许有人知道该怎么做。
谢谢

大卫塞拉诺卡纳莱斯 - Apr 16 4月16日



原文:

I'm developing my library for react-native. I need to use camera.
I found nice packages react-native-camera-kit and react-native-vision-camera
I created my lib using create-react-native-library (https://github.com/callstack/react-native-builder-bob)

Added react-native-camera-kit to devDependencies and peerDependencies. Added and configured react-native-camera-kit to my app

When I'm using react-native-camera-kit inside my app everything is ok. I created component with react-native-camera-kit inside my lib and export it.

When I'm trying to use this component I'm getting an error Camera is closed

Again. Using react-native-camera-kit directly inside app - OK. Using react-native-camera-kit through my lib - Error

Maybe somebody knows what to do.
Thanks

David Serrano Canales - Apr 16
item.author: ntf23

item.title: AWS S3 安全性 | AWS S3 Security
item.description:
默认情况下，所有存储桶和对象都是私有的。 只有资源所有者（即创建它的 AWS 账户）才能访问该资源。

可以通过以下方式控制对 S3 存储桶和/或对象的访问

  1. 桶策略
  2. 访问控制列表

桶策略

存储桶策略附加到 S3 存储桶并定义允许或拒绝谁

什么时候用什么？

有关 AWS 的更多此类简单说明，请关注我

领英： https ://www.linkedin.com/in/venkatesh111/

推特： https ://twitter.com/venkatesh111

鲁斯兰·艾哈迈齐亚诺夫—— Jun 14 6月14日



原文:

All Buckets and objects are private by default. Only the resource owner, an AWS account that created it, can access the resource.

Access to S3 bucket and/or objects can be controlled via

  1. IAM Policies
  2. Bucket Polices
  3. Access Control Lists

IAM Policies, Bucket Polices, & ACL

IAM Policies Vs. Bucket Polices

IAM Policies are attached to IAM Entities such as User, Groups and Roles and they define What is allowed or denied ?

Bucket Polices are attached to S3 Buckets and define Who is allowed or denied

AWS S3 Block Public Access Block Public Access is applicable to only Public/Anonymous access Block public access settings can override ACLs and bucket policies public access You can apply block public access settings to individual buckets or to all buckets in your account

When to use What ?

AWS References:

https://aws.amazon.com/s3/security/

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/s3-access-control.html

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/using-iam-policies.html

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/acls.html

https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/access-control-block-public-access.html

For More such simple notes on AWS, Follow me on

LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/venkatesh111/

Twitter: https://twitter.com/venkatesh111

GitHub: https://venkateshk111.github.io/

Ruslan Akhmetzianov - Jun 14
item.author: venkatesh111

item.title: 克里皮奥 | Klippyo
item.description:
用户可以从他们的移动设备创建视频，并在他们舒适的手掌上编辑、发布和分享所有内容。

如果您的客户更喜欢在他们的笔记本电脑/PC 上制作视频，那没问题。 不仅如此，如果您的用户不想拍摄任何视频，他们还可以利用庞大的视频资源库来创建自己的视频。

视频内容现在以完全不同的方式主导社交媒体。 意识到这一点的人们正在创建非常受欢迎的频道，拥有更多的粉丝和订阅者，这比看起来公平的要多！

但这只是因为人们认为内容创建很容易，而事实并非如此。 拍摄、编辑和发布新鲜内容是一项复杂且耗时的任务。 要引起注意是一场持续的斗争。 因此，那些努力获得关注的人应该得到所有的粉丝、所有的赞誉以及他们因此获得的所有销售额。

问题是视频已经发生了很大的变化，以至于许多人完全没有得到任何牵引力。 这不是因为缺乏努力，或者糟糕的内容，或者你通常会想到的任何原因。 你可以拥有世界上最好的内容，但它仍然不会被看到。

这完全是因为视频的使用方式不同。 好消息是，一旦您知道有什么不同，做出改变并获得回报就很容易了。 结果可能是惊人的：

参与度提高 80-100%。 视频观看次数增加 30-35%。 每次参与的成本降低 33%。 新闻源中的可见性提高 78%。

真正的好消息是，刚刚推出了一款名为 Klippyo 的新应用程序，任何人都可以毫不费力地进行这些更改。 这是一个可靠的应用程序，由与索尼、特斯拉和沃达丰等品牌合作过的团队创建。

它甚至被 Derral 自己使用。 在顶部，添加表情符号、图形、图像甚至字幕。 这是视频创作的未来。

用简单的 3 个步骤创建具有高度吸引力的视频的更快、最简单和最智能的方法：

第一步：挑选。 射击。 进口

第 2 步：自定义

第 3 步：发布

报价页面上有更多详细信息，Klippyo 绝对值得您花时间。 如果没有其他原因，只是想看看发生了什么变化以及您需要对视频做些什么才能从现在开始取得成功。 立即获取。

使用 Klippyo，您可以在几秒钟内轻松地 meme'ify 您的视频！ 在几秒钟内添加表情符号、文本、报价和更多内容：想为您的视频添加一点额外的活力吗？ 只需点击几个按钮，即可轻松快速地添加表情符号、文本、绘图甚至流行语录。 无需复杂的软件，一切都在您的手掌中发生。 添加 1 Tap 动画简介和结尾：Klippyo 允许用户为您的视频添加完全可定制的简介和结尾！ 想要在视频开头播放时髦的文字？ 需要在视频末尾显示您的社交链接？ 没问题。 需要方形或矩形视频？ 您在几秒钟内就搞定了：在点击性能和盈利能力方面，垂直和方形视频已经多次证明了它们的重要性！ 问题是，手动执行此操作是有问题且耗时的。 不再。 自动字幕：统计证明，带字幕的视频具有更高的参与度和保留率。 值得庆幸的是，使用 Klippyo 添加字幕只需点击 1 个按钮，就像魔术一样，Klippyo 可以在几分钟内为您的视频添加字幕！ 或者，您也可以上传您的字幕文件。 拍摄、编辑和点击发布：完成您的视频了吗？ 好的。 无需渲染大量视频文件。 只需点击“发布”并将您的视频发布到您想要的任何社交网络……以您想要的任何分辨率或比例！ 没有自己的视频？ 没问题：Klippyo 与 Viddyoze Stock 完全集成，让用户可以访问数以千计的库存视频剪辑来创建他们的视频！ 无需拍摄任何东西！ 只需从我们庞大的库存库中挑选一个专业视频，自定义并制作您自己的视频，然后在完成后发布！

非凡的客户支持。 桌面软件绑定到您的计算机？ 这意味着您可以在几分钟内随时随地拍摄视频、编辑它们并发布到任何社交网络，而无需昂贵的桌面软件。

社交视频模因可从任何平台上的移动新闻源中产生惊人的效果。 多个纵横比。 以适合您的内容、平台或设备的任何宽高比进行拍摄、编辑和发布。 专为移动设备打造的专业编辑套件，让您可以随时随地制作精彩的社交视频。 直接社交媒体整合：直接分享到您最喜欢的社交平台。 表情符号和贴纸：鼓励喜欢、分享和许多其他反应，以将更多人带入您的社交影响范围。 介绍和结尾：可定制的介绍和结尾，以吸引注意力，创建简单的品牌，并通过直接号召采取行动来激发行动。 字幕：提高观众的留存率，并与听障人士、安静环境中的人以及 85% 关闭声音观看视频的 Facebook 用户互动。 引语：引语像其他任何东西一样病毒式传播，Klippyo 让您可以快速轻松地找到引语并直接从您的手机发布。

查看 Klippyo



原文:

KLIPPYO WHAT IS IT
lippyo is a web based video creator that allows users to create high quality, engaging video content in just a few minutes. Users can create videos from their mobile device, and edit, publish & share all from the comfort of their palm.

lf your customers prefer making videos on their laptop/PC, no problem. Klippyo is 100% cloud based, allowing users to create from anywhere. Not only that, but if your users do not want to shoot any video, they have the ability to tap into a massive library of video assets to create their.

Video content is now dominating social media in a completely different way. And the people who’ve realised this are creating hugely popular channels with more fans and subscribers than seems fair!

But that’s only because people think content creation is easy, and it’s not. Shooting, editing, and publishing fresh content is a complex, time-consuming task. It’s a constant struggle to get noticed. So the people that put in the effort to get noticed deserve all the fans, all the accolades, and all the sales they get as a result.

The problem is video has changed so much that many people are completely failing to get any traction at all. And it’s not because of a lack of effort, or bad content, or any reason you’d typically think of. You could have the best content in the world, and it’ll still not get seen.

And it’s all because of the different way video is being used. The good news is once you know what’s so different, making the change and reaping the rewards is easy. And the results can be stunning:

80-100% more engagement. 30-35% higher video views. 33% less cost per engagement. 78% more visibility in newsfeeds.

And the really good news is that there’s a new application that’s just launched called Klippyo that lets anyone make these changes with next no effort at all. This is a solid app, created by a team who’ve worked with brands like Sony, Tesla, and Vodaphone.

Klippyo is helping everyday content creators create video that’s proven to be more engaging, capable of generating cheaper clicks and making your brand 78% more visible on the news feed. It’s even used by Derral himself. Klippyo also features the ability to add intros & outros (straight from Viddyoze), lower thirds & overlays with just a click of a mouse. On top, add emojis, graphics, images & even captions. Klippyo features truly endless amounts of customization for your users video content. This is the future of video creation.

The Faster, Easiest & Smartest Way To Create Highly Engaging Videos with easy 3 steps:

Step 1: Pick. Shoot. Import

Step 2: Customize

Step 3: Publish

There’s a ton more detail on the offer page, and Klippyo is definitely worth your time. If for no other reason than to see what’s changed and what you need to be doing with your videos to succeed from here on out. Get It Now.

KLIPPYO FEATURES Meme Your Video In Just 1 Tap: Video memes aren’t just the trendy new thing to do… they are proven to increase clicks, conversions and engagement! With Klippyo, you can easily meme’ify your video in seconds! Add Emojis, Text, Quotes & More In Seconds: Want to add a little extra zing to your videos? Easily and quickly add emojis, text, drawings or even popular quotes by simply tapping a few buttons. No complex software necessary, everything happens from the palm of your hand. Add 1 Tap Animated Intros & Outros: Klippyo allows users to add fully customisable intros & outros to your videos! Want a funky text call out at the beginning of your video? Need to show your social links at the end of your video? No problemo. Viddyoze Integration: Connect to Klippyo on a web browser & import your videos directly to your Klippyo creations! Need A Square Or Rectangular Video? You’ve Got It In Seconds: Vertical & square videos have proven their weight in gold many times over when it comes to click performance & profitability! The problem is, doing this manually is problematic and time consuming. Not anymore. Klippyo taps the problem away with just 1 prod of your finger. Automatic Captions: It’s been statistically proven that captioned videos have much higher engagement and retention rates. Thankfully, adding captions with Klippyo is just a case of tapping 1 button, and like magic, Klippyo can add captions to your video in minutes! Alternatively, you can upload your on caption files too. Shoot, Edit & Tap To Publish: Finished your video? Good. No need to render extensive video files. Simply hit “Publish” and post your video to any social network you desire… in any resolution or ratio you want! Don’t Have Your Own Video? No Problem: Klippyo is fully integrated with Viddyoze Stock, giving users access to thousands of stock video clips to create their videos from! No need to shoot anything! Simply pick a professional video from our massive stock library, customize and make it your own, then publish when you’re done!

100% Cloud Based. Phenomenal Customer Support. Desktop software tied to your computer? Klippyo works on any web browser on any portable device! That means you can shoot videos on the go, edit them, and publish to any social network all within minutes, without the need of expensive desktop software.

**WHAT KLIPPYO CAN DO FOR YOU **Square videos give you 78% more screen real-estate, increase views, engagement, and reduce your cost per engagement on mobile platforms. Social video memes produce stunning results from mobile newsfeeds on any platform. Multiple aspect ratios. Shoot, edit and publish in any aspect ratio that suits your content, platform, or device. Professional editing suite purpose-built for mobile so you can create incredible social videos on the go. Direct social media integration: Share straight to your favourite social platforms. Emojis and Stickers: encourage likes, shares, and many other reactions to bring more people into your social sphere of influence. Intros and Outros: customizable intros and outros to grab attention, create easy branding, and inspire action with direct calls to action. Intros and Outros Pro: Integrate your Viddyoze account to bring world-class video animations to your Klippyo content. Captions: boost viewer retention and interact with the hearing impaired, people in quiet environments, and the 85% of Facebook users who watch videos with the sound off. Captions Pro as an entirely optional extra, the team at Klippyo will create and sync pro-level captions for your video, for a small fee. Quotes: quotes go viral like nothing else, and Klippyo makes it fast and easy to find a quote and publish it directly from your phone.

Check out The Klippyo

item.title: 作为 27 岁的失业者，我学到的五件事 | Five things I have learned being a jobless 27 year old
item.description:
克里斯蒂娜·帕切科

发表于有趣的工作已经 13 天 298 小时 17,930 分钟。 我从来没有想过在我职业生涯的巅峰时期，我会在 27 岁时失业。 生活，伙计。 它毫不留情。

此外，在求职过程中，我学到了一些东西：

  1. 随时更新您的LinkedIn！

  2. 选择一家符合你的价值观和道德的公司。

  3. 不要让冒名顶替综合症入侵！！

  4. 打扫房间！ 不要害怕利用您的网络

  5. 做你自己。 简单的。

如果您最近经历过裁员或被取消的报价，请先照顾好自己的心理，继续前进并了解更多信息。

请回来查看下周的帖子！



原文:

Christina Pacheco

Posted on Jun 14

It has been 13 days, 298 hours and 17,930 minutes of being fun-employed. Never did I think at 27 years old at the peak of my career I would be jobless. Life, man. It holds no mercy.

Furthermore, on my job seeking journey I have learned a couple of things:

  1. Always get your LinkedIn updated!

  2. Pick a company that aligns with your values and morals.

  3. Do NOT let imposter syndrome invade!!

  4. Werk the room! Do not be afraid to leverage your network

  5. Be yourself. simple.

If you have recently experienced a layoff or rescinded offer, take care of your mental first, keep going and learn more.

Check back for next weeks post!
